sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CenterPoint Energy (CNP) Stock Rises Amid Storm Outage Forecast
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy (CNP) saw its stock rise by 1.8% after forecasting potential power outages affecting 100,000 to 200,000 customers due to an impending storm. The utility company reassured customers that power restoration for most affected individuals would occur within 12 hours. This timely market reporting was generated using automated technology and GuruFocus financial data.

feed.title: Halliburton EVP Beckwith sells $1.9 million in shares
feed.summary: Halliburton's Executive Vice President, Secretary, and CLO, Beckwith Van H., sold 54,348 shares of company stock for $1.9 million after exercising options. The transaction occurred as Halliburton shares traded near their 52-week high, following strong fourth-quarter 2025 earnings that surpassed analyst expectations. Analysts maintain a positive outlook for the energy services company, citing its financial stability and performance.

feed.title: Despit running the $75 billion automaker General Motors, its CEO Mary Barra still responds to ‘every single letter’ she gets by hand
feed.summary: General Motors CEO Mary Barra, who leads a $75 billion automaker, personally responds to every letter she receives, from appreciative customers to those with grievances. This practice highlights her commitment to direct communication and human connection, a habit she's maintained throughout her career at GM. Other executives, like First Watch CEO Chris Tomasso and Saks CEO Geoffroy van Raemdonck, also prioritize handwritten notes and personalized outreach to foster loyalty and appreciation among employees and customers.

feed.title: KeyCorp (NYSE:KEY) Price Target Raised to $23.00 at Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial has increased its price target for KeyCorp (NYSE:KEY) from $22.00 to $23.00, maintaining a "hold" rating, suggesting an 8.67% upside. This adjustment follows KeyCorp's recent outperformance in quarterly earnings, where it reported $0.41 EPS against an expected $0.39 and revenues of $2.01 billion, surpassing estimates. Analyst sentiment is mixed, with the stock holding an average "Hold" rating and a consensus price target of $22.68 across various firms.

feed.title: Chipotle’s CMO Chris Brandt to Step Down
feed.summary: Chipotle's Chief Marketing Officer, Chris Brandt, is stepping down after leading the company's marketing efforts since 2018. During his tenure, Chipotle's market cap grew significantly, and he spearheaded various campaigns and partnerships, including a recent collaboration with the PGA TOUR, to drive customer engagement. Stephanie Perdue, VP of Brand Marketing, will serve as interim CMO as the company searches for a permanent replacement.

feed.title: Fiserv And Affirm Will Offer BNPL to Debit Card holders
feed.summary: Affirm Holdings Inc. has partnered with Fiserv Inc. to expand the availability of its buy now, pay later (BNPL) loan programs to debit card holders. This collaboration will allow banks on Fiserv's network to integrate Affirm's pay-over-time solution into their debit card offerings, enabling customers to request BNPL loans for purchases directly through their financial institutions' mobile apps. The initiative aims to help banks retain transactions and engagement within their ecosystem, preventing them from being lost to third-party BNPL providers.

feed.title: Alphabet Class C (GOOG) stock price today: What’s moving Google shares ahead of the Fed and earnings
feed.summary: Alphabet's Class C shares (GOOG) saw a 0.9% rise, reaching $331.28, amidst a stronger U.S. equities market. Investors are closely watching the upcoming Federal Reserve decision and Alphabet's earnings report scheduled for February 4. The company faces ongoing regulatory scrutiny, including a new legal settlement regarding Google Assistant's privacy and a lawsuit against YouTube concerning youth addiction.

feed.title: Duke Energy expects Sumter, Lee, Clarendon Counties service restored by 3 p.m., encourages outage reporting, warns against scams
feed.summary: Duke Energy anticipates restoring service to Sumter, Lee, and Clarendon Counties by 3 p.m. Monday, January 26, 2026. The company is encouraging customers to report any outages and has issued a warning against potential scams. Active outages in Sumter County significantly decreased according to Duke Energy's records between 9:05 a.m. and 10:31 a.m. on the same day.

feed.title: TD Cowen Bullish on Constellation Energy (CEG) Amid Heightened Regulatory Risks and Recently Completed Calpine Acquisition
feed.summary: TD Cowen has initiated coverage on Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG) with a 'Buy' rating and a $440 price target, despite acknowledging heightened regulatory risks. The firm views the recently completed $16.4 billion Calpine acquisition as a significant growth catalyst and highlights potential upside from gas PPA agreements and CEG's nuclear fleet. Management anticipates the Calpine deal will add approximately $2 billion in annual free cash flow and contribute 55 GW of zero- and low-emission capacity.

feed.title: Micron Technology Stock Slips on Nvidia-Samsung Supply Deal
feed.summary: Micron Technology's shares dropped 2.3% after news broke that Samsung would supply Nvidia with HBM4 memory chips, impacting Micron's stock despite a recent record high. The stock has seen a significant increase from its April 7 low, and options traders are actively targeting MU, with a high Schaeffer's Volatility Scorecard indicating a tendency to exceed volatility expectations.

feed.title: Broadcom Holds 18-Month Edge In Custom AI Chips, 'Buy Aggressively:' JPMorgan Analyst
feed.summary: JPMorgan analyst Harlan Sur reiterates an Overweight rating and a $475 price target on Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO), citing its significant lead in custom AI chips, particularly with Alphabet Inc (NASDAQ: GOOGL) as a key client. Broadcom's next-gen 3nm Tensor Processing Unit (TPU), codenamed Sunfish, gives it a substantial timing advantage over Google's internal efforts, leading to billions in purchase orders and expected widespread adoption within Google's AI infrastructure by 2027. Sur recommends buying Broadcom shares aggressively due to this enduring competitive edge and strong growth prospects.

feed.title: Northern Trust stock price target raised to $175 by TD Cowen
feed.summary: TD Cowen increased its price target for Northern Trust (NASDAQ:NTRS) to $175 from $165, maintaining a Buy rating after the financial services company's strong fourth-quarter 2025 earnings. The company's core EPS of $2.62 exceeded expectations due to robust pre-tax pre-provision income and lower provision expense. Northern Trust also raised its medium-term pre-tax margin and return on equity targets, reinforcing confidence in its long-term profitability.

feed.title: Brokers Suggest Investing in PulteGroup (PHM): Read This Before Placing a Bet
feed.summary: This article examines brokerage recommendations for PulteGroup (PHM), which currently holds an average "Buy" rating from 17 firms. However, it cautions investors against relying solely on these recommendations due to potential positive bias from brokerage firms' vested interests. The article suggests validating such recommendations with tools like the Zacks Rank, which for PulteGroup is currently a #4 (Sell) due to declining earnings estimates.

feed.title: CFG Stock Rating Update: Citigroup Raises Price Target to $71
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Citizens Financial Group (CFG) to $71 from $65, while maintaining a 'Buy' rating. This update follows a trend of positive sentiment from several analysts, who have also recently increased their price targets for CFG. The average target price from 19 analysts is $71.84, suggesting a 14.61% upside from the current price, though GuruFocus' GF Value indicates a potential downside.

feed.title: Charles Schwab (SCHW) Target Price Raised by Barclays to $126
feed.summary: Barclays has raised its target price for Charles Schwab (SCHW) to $126 from $125, maintaining an "Overweight" rating. This increase reflects continued confidence in the company's market performance, following similar positive adjustments from other analysts earlier in January. Schwab, a major financial services provider with $11.9 trillion in client assets, generates significant revenue from its banking and asset management segments.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adds AWM Partners to Management Committee
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs announced the addition of several Asset & Wealth Management (AWM) partners to its Management Committee, effective today. Chairman and CEO David Solomon highlighted that these leaders bring extensive experience and expertise to their new roles. This move aims to advance the firm's core strategic objective of growing and building a global, scaled AWM platform.

feed.title: Fidelity National Information Services stock hits 52-week low at $59.49
feed.summary: Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) stock has dropped to a new 52-week low of $59.49, reflecting a 26.54% decline over the past year and a 25.28% drop in the last six months. Despite this downturn, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock may be undervalued and oversold, offering a 2.64% dividend yield with 23 consecutive years of dividend payments. The company recently exceeded Q3 2025 earnings expectations, completed a significant acquisition and sale in its Issuer Solutions business and Worldpay, respectively, and appointed a new board member.

feed.title: BlackRock Private Debt Fund Tumbles After Writing Down Loans
feed.summary: A BlackRock Inc. private debt fund, BlackRock TCP Capital Corp., experienced its largest plunge in nearly six years after announcing significant writedowns on troubled investments. The writedowns are expected to reduce its net asset value by 19% as of the end of last year, leading to a 16.7% stock drop and a fee waiver.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial Has Lowered Expectations for Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) Stock Price
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has slightly lowered its price target for Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) from $825 to $800, while maintaining a "strong-buy" rating, indicating an 18.99% potential upside. Despite a revenue beat and strong fundamentals, Meta faces risks from recent insider stock sales and ongoing litigation/regulatory challenges, including a youth-addiction trial. Other analysts have also adjusted their price targets for Meta, with the consensus remaining a "Moderate Buy" at $820.70.

feed.title: Here's Why I Wouldn't Touch SoundHound AI With a 10-Foot Pole
feed.summary: The author advises caution against investing in SoundHound AI (SOUN), citing its lack of profitability despite offering interesting voice-based AI services. The article highlights the stock's high volatility, with two 50% drops in the past year, and questions its long-term viability in a competitive AI landscape, suggesting that an AI-focused ETF might be a safer alternative for investors seeking exposure to the technology.

feed.title: KBR (NYSE:KBR) Price Target Cut to $53.00 by Analysts at Citigroup
feed.summary: Citigroup analysts have reduced their price target for KBR (NYSE:KBR) from $57.00 to $53.00, while maintaining a "buy" rating, indicating a potential upside of 22.53%. This adjustment comes despite KBR beating its last quarterly EPS estimates. The stock currently has a "Hold" consensus rating among analysts, with a target price of $57.00.

feed.title: Customers Bancorp (NYSE:CUBI) Price Target Raised to $95.00
feed.summary: DA Davidson raised its price target for Customers Bancorp (NYSE:CUBI) to $95.00 from $93.00, reiterating a "buy" rating, indicating a potential upside of 23.55%. This follows Customers Bancorp's strong Q4 earnings, where it beat EPS and revenue estimates. Despite recent insider selling, institutional ownership remains high at 89.29%.

feed.title: Intuitive’s cardiac initiative includes valve repair, LAA closure
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical has announced U.S. clearance for nine new cardiac procedures for its da Vinci 5 robot, including mitral and tricuspid valve repair, mitral valve replacement, and left atrial appendage closure. The company plans a limited U.S. launch in 2026, working with select sites to establish cardiac programs, and is pursuing approvals in Europe and other regions. This move marks a recommitment to cardiac surgery, addressing previous technical limitations and support infrastructure gaps that had shifted its focus away from the specialty.

feed.title: Earnings Preview For PPG Indus
feed.summary: PPG Industries (NYSE: PPG) is set to release its quarterly earnings report on January 27, 2026. Analysts expect an EPS of $1.58. The article provides an overview of past earnings performance, stock performance, analyst observations, and a comparison with peers like Albemarle, Intl Flavors & Fragrances, and DuPont de Nemours.

feed.title: Why Analysts Are Closely Watching Wells Fargo & Company (WFC)
feed.summary: Analysts are closely watching Wells Fargo & Company (WFC) after recent price target cuts by TD Cowen and Truist Securities, despite impressive underlying organic growth. These revisions were primarily driven by a projected 2-3% decline in EPS for 2026 and 2027 due to estimated higher fee income and increased costs. The company, headquartered in San Francisco, is a financial services giant with four main segments, aiming to satisfy clients' financial needs.

feed.title: Microsoft Unveils Latest AI Chip to Reduce Reliance on Nvidia
feed.summary: Microsoft has launched its second-generation artificial intelligence chip, the Maia 200, to enhance the efficiency of its services and decrease its dependence on Nvidia's hardware. Produced by TSMC, the chip is currently being deployed in Microsoft data centers in Iowa and will eventually reach the Phoenix area. While developers can now access Maia's control software, its availability for Azure cloud service users is yet to be announced.

feed.title: Gilead Sciences stock price target raised to $150 by BMO Capital
feed.summary: BMO Capital has increased its price target for Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) to $150 from $135, maintaining an Outperform rating due to strong momentum in the company's HIV business, particularly the successful launch and coverage of Yeztugo. The firm also highlighted Gilead's under-the-radar advancements in obesity and Type 2 diabetes and its financial flexibility for strategic acquisitions. Other analysts like Bernstein, UBS, and BofA Securities have also reiterated positive ratings, citing consistent strength across Gilead's product portfolio and recent positive clinical trial results.

feed.title: Local e-commerce platform raises US$258 million
feed.summary: Vietnamese e-commerce platform Tiki has secured $258 million in a Series E funding round led by AIA, boosting its valuation to nearly $1 billion. The company plans to expand into the life insurance business, leverage the new capital for logistics improvements, and potentially pursue an IPO in the US sooner than its original 2025 target. This investment highlights global confidence in Vietnam's growing tech sector and could pave the way for more foreign investment.

feed.title: Gartner: Humaniod Robots Will Not Join Global Supply Chains
feed.summary: Gartner predicts that despite innovation, humanoid robots will not be widely adopted in global supply chains, with fewer than 20 companies scaling them by 2028. The firm highlights that technological limitations, integration complexity, high costs, and energy constraints prevent widespread deployment. Instead, Gartner suggests polyfunctional robots, which offer better efficiency and adaptability for warehouse operations, are a superior solution for supply chain needs.

feed.title: Should You Invest in TJX (TJX) Based on Bullish Wall Street Views?
feed.summary: This article examines the reliability of Wall Street analyst recommendations for investing in TJX (TJX). While TJX currently holds a strong "Buy" average brokerage recommendation based on 21 firms, the article cautions against relying solely on these recommendations due to inherent biases. It instead promotes using the proprietary Zacks Rank, which is based on earnings estimate revisions and is presented as a more reliable indicator for near-term stock performance.

feed.title: Power restored to more than 85% of Dominion Energy customers, Monday update says
feed.summary: Dominion Energy has restored power to over 85% of customers affected by a recent winter storm, bringing the total number of impacted customers down from about 48,000. Despite progress, strong winds and ice could cause further outages, and challenging road conditions are hindering restoration efforts in some areas. The company assures customers that crews are working diligently around the clock to restore power safely and quickly.

feed.title: Brokers Suggest Investing in Micron (MU): Read This Before Placing a Bet
feed.summary: This article discusses the average brokerage recommendation (ABR) for Micron (MU), which is 1.29 (between Strong Buy and Buy) based on 41 firms. However, it cautions against relying solely on ABRs due to brokerage firms' inherent positive bias, suggesting instead that investors use it to validate their own research or a more reliable indicator like the Zacks Rank. The article highlights that Micron currently holds a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) due to increasing earnings estimates, making the Buy-equivalent ABR a useful guide when combined with this information.

feed.title: Mevotech Receives Two Supplier Awards From O’Reilly - The BRAKE Report
feed.summary: Mevotech, a North American manufacturer of steering, suspension, braking parts, and wheel hub assemblies, has received two supplier awards from O'Reilly Auto Parts: the Strategic Partnership Award and the Content & Catalog Award. These awards recognize Mevotech's exceptional operational performance, collaborative efforts, and product data accuracy, which support O'Reilly's omnichannel retail operations. The company, established in 1982, specializes in aftermarket automotive components and has its headquarters in Toronto, Ontario.

feed.title: FFIV Investors Have Opportunity to Lead F5, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm
feed.summary: The Schall Law Firm is reminding investors of a class action lawsuit against F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) for alleged securities fraud. The firm states that F5 made false and misleading statements regarding its security and ability to meet customer needs, particularly after a security incident put customers and growth prospects at risk. Investors who purchased F5 securities between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025, are encouraged to contact The Schall Law Firm by February 17, 2026, to discuss their rights and participate in the lawsuit to recover losses.

feed.title: Elon Musk Reveals When Tesla Will Sell an Item That 'Everyone on Earth' Will Eventually Own
feed.summary: Elon Musk announced that Tesla plans to make its Optimus humanoid robots available for public sale by the end of 2027, predicting they will become as common as smartphones. He believes these robots will be highly reliable, safe, functional, and capable of performing a wide range of tasks, including household chores and caregiving. While early production will be slow, Musk envisions a future where "everyone on Earth" owns one, despite current technical challenges and investor calls for scalable manufacturing proof.

feed.title: Sysco Corp. (SYY) call put ratio 1 call to 4.6 puts into quarter results
feed.summary: Sysco Corp. (SYY) is showing a call-put ratio of 1 call to 4.6 puts ahead of its upcoming quarter results, which are expected before the bell on January 27. The February weekly call option implied volatility is 24, and the March implied volatility is 20, set against a 52-week range of 15 to 39. The company's stock price is $76.36, with an overall analyst rating of SELL, a dividend yield of 2.9%, and EPS growth of 5.4%.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) Sees Analyst Rating Boost from DA Dav
feed.summary: DA Davidson has maintained its 'Buy' rating for Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) and raised its price target from $54.00 to $58.00, indicating a positive outlook. This update follows several other analysts who have also increased their price targets for FITB recently. Analysts' consensus suggests an average target price of $56.91, implying an upside of 12.76% from the current price.

feed.title: GM Just Fixed One Of The Biggest Headaches Of EV Charging
feed.summary: General Motors has integrated over 5,000 Electrify America DC fast chargers into its native smartphone apps (myChevrolet, myGMC, myCadillac), allowing EV owners to find, initiate, and pay for charging directly through a single app. This integration, along with others including Ionna and Tesla Superchargers, grants GM EV drivers access to over 250,000 public chargers across the U.S. and Canada, simplifying the charging experience by reducing the need for multiple apps.

feed.title: Tesla Q4 Preview: This May Disappoint, But The Autonomy Talk May Move It Anyway (TSLA)
feed.summary: The author is downgrading Tesla (TSLA) to a Hold ahead of its Q4 earnings report, citing weak EV delivery fundamentals with a 15.6% YOY decline in Q4 and a second consecutive year of overall delivery decline. While valuation is considered extreme at 350x forward earnings, the article suggests that discussions around monetizing Full Self-Driving, FSD approvals in Europe/China, and robotaxi rollout details could still influence the stock. The author, a small deep value individual investor, maintains a 12-18 month Hold perspective rather than a Buy.

feed.title: Wahlburgers plots expansion at Home Depot, Bass Pro Shops
feed.summary: Wahlburgers is accelerating its growth in 2026 by focusing on non-traditional locations such as airports, hotels, Home Depot, and Bass Pro Shops. This strategy aims to rebuild its store count after the termination of a partnership with Hy-Vee, which resulted in the closure of 79 restaurants. The burger chain plans to utilize Wahlburger trailers at Home Depot locations and open full restaurants within Bass Pro Shops stores, alongside continued expansion at airports and other venues.

feed.title: Bank of America Corporation (BAC) Evaluates New Credit Cards With 10% Rate
feed.summary: Bank of America (BAC) is considering introducing new credit cards with a 10% interest rate to comply with President Donald Trump's directives. This news follows a recent price target trimming by TD Cowen to $64 from $66, despite BAC surpassing Q4 2025 earnings expectations. The bank's stock has seen a slight decline since its earnings announcement, attributed to near-term operating leverage projections.

feed.title: Winter Storm Causes Massive Flight Cancellations
feed.summary: A severe winter storm has led to the cancellation of over 15,000 U.S. flights, including more than 4,000 on Monday morning alone, making it the largest cancellation event since early 2020. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy expects flights to normalize by Wednesday, though the FAA will need additional time for full recovery due to ice, snow, and staffing shortages. Major airlines like American Airlines and Delta Air Lines are waiving rebooking fees for affected passengers.

feed.title: Enphase Energy to lay off ~160 employees as part of broader restructuring (ENPH:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Enphase Energy (ENPH) is implementing a restructuring plan that includes laying off approximately 160 employees, representing about 6% of its workforce. This decision follows a decrease in demand for residential solar panels due to the expiration of a federal tax credit. The company's stock was down 0.9% in early trading following the announcement.

feed.title: Can Oracle's FedRAMP Push Unlock Federal Cloud Opportunities?
feed.summary: Oracle is strategically targeting federal cloud opportunities by pursuing FedRAMP authorization for its Primavera Cloud platform, which specializes in project management for government infrastructure. This move aims to leverage Oracle's niche in complex federal projects, allowing it to compete with established federal cloud providers like Amazon, Microsoft, and Salesforce. By meeting stringent compliance requirements, Oracle seeks to secure long-term revenue streams within the government sector.

feed.title: Medtronic wins CE mark for single-shot pulsed field ablation catheter
feed.summary: Medtronic has received a CE mark for its Affera Sphere-360 pulsed field ablation catheter for paroxysmal atrial fibrillation. This single-shot device competes directly with Boston Scientific's Farawave catheter, distinguishing itself by not requiring rotation during ablation. Medtronic, which saw a 300% increase in PFA sales in its second quarter, is also conducting a clinical trial to secure U.S. authorization for Sphere-360.

feed.title: Stocks flashing renewed technical strength: Gilead Sciences
feed.summary: The article identifies Gilead Sciences (GILD) as a stock demonstrating renewed technical strength. The analysis is based on a proprietary scoring system that evaluates key technical indicators for potential upward momentum.

feed.title: Home Depot continues AI-enabling pro customers with list builder tool
feed.summary: Home Depot has launched Material List Builder AI, a new solution designed to help professional renovators, remodelers, builders, and tradespeople quickly create project material lists. The AI tool interprets project intent from various inputs (natural language, voice-to-text, existing lists) and generates organized, editable material lists with product recommendations, pricing, and inventory availability. This initiative is part of Home Depot's ongoing efforts to provide AI-powered solutions and specialized services to its pro customers.

feed.title: Halliburton Prepares for Quick Comeback in Venezuela With US Approval
feed.summary: Halliburton (HAL) is preparing for a swift return to Venezuela, pending U.S. government approval and payment safeguards, to capitalize on the country's vast oil reserves. This move aims to offset weakening demand in the U.S. shale market and is part of Halliburton's broader Latin American growth strategy, which also includes Brazil, Argentina, Ecuador, and Guyana. Other major players like Chevron, ExxonMobil, and ConocoPhillips are also eyeing Venezuela’s oil sector as political shifts and potential easing of sanctions create new opportunities.

feed.title: Can Oracle's FedRAMP Push Unlock Federal Cloud Opportunities?
feed.summary: Oracle is strategically targeting the federal cloud market by pushing its Primavera Cloud platform toward FedRAMP Moderate authorization. This certification is crucial for securing government contracts, which offer stable, long-term revenue. While facing strong competition from Amazon, Microsoft, and Salesforce, Oracle aims to differentiate itself through specialized project management software for complex federal infrastructure projects, capitalizing on unique compliance needs.

feed.title: How Franchise Economics Can Fuel YUM's Next Growth Cycle
feed.summary: Yum! Brands (YUM) is focusing on improving franchise-level economics to drive its next growth phase, aiming to make unit expansion and long-term value creation attractive to franchise partners. This strategy involves leveraging scale through global purchasing power, supply-chain coordination, and technology like the Byte platform to increase profitability for franchisees. The company's approach, including the creation of a chief scale officer role, aims to create a self-reinforcing growth loop, positioning YUM favorably against competitors like McDonald's and Restaurant Brands International.
